name: ti_sweep_1
description: First TI sweep. n4_baseline (suffix, batch=16, lr=1e-3) completed — loss 1.025→0.963, plateau after step 1500, token_norm grew linearly without saturation (overshoot sign). Now testing: prefix injection, lower LR, smaller batch.

experiments.description: COMPLETED. batch=16, lr=1e-3, suffix. Reference. Loss plateau ~0.963, token_norm linear growth to 3.2 — LR too high for the parameter count.
Same as baseline but prefix injection. Tests whether suffix positions are limiting signal — if prefix loss goes lower or converges faster, suffix was the bottleneck.
lr=2e-4, batch=4. Matches LoRA's working regime. Smaller batch = noisier but more diverse gradients; lower LR = smaller steps, token_norm should plateau rather than drift.
lr=5e-4, batch=8. Middle ground — half the baseline LR and batch. Token norm should grow slower and saturate.
lr=2e-4, batch=4, prefix. Best LR/batch regime + best injection position combined.
8 tokens, prefix, baseline LR/batch. More capacity at the better injection position.
4 tokens, prefix, warm-started from 'mechanical impact sound design'.
8 tokens, suffix, baseline LR/batch. Capacity ablation vs n4_baseline.
lr=2e-3, baseline batch. Expected to plateau earlier and higher than baseline — confirms LR is the issue.
